title: Wait Node URL becomes invalid without webhook suffix
symptom: When trying to access the wait node form URL after sending it via email, the link indicates that it is no longer valid, causing the workflow to remain in a waiting state indefinitely.
applies_when: Using the Wait node with a configured webhook suffix
Sending the resume form URL via email
not_this_if: The workflow is executed manually without sending the URL
root_cause: The resume form URL generated by the Wait node does not automatically include any configured webhook suffix. This means that if the URL is sent elsewhere without the suffix, it will not be valid when accessed later, leading to unexpected behavior.
fix_steps: Manually concatenate the configured webhook suffix to the resume form URL before sending it out.
Ensure that the complete URL is sent to the intended recipient.
agent_instruction: Always include the webhook suffix when sending the resume form URL from the Wait node.
